title: gladiolus ecklonii subsp. rehmannii
intro: Gladiolus ecklonii subsp. rehmannii is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the Iridaceae family, commonly known as the gladiolus or corn lily. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gladiolus ecklonii subsp. rehmannii include reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in water, and it is often used in traditional remedies in southern Africa.
